label: performance_quadratic
summary: The logic is correct — case normalization, empty-input handling, and the count >= 2 gate all work. However, the implementation uses a nested loop with an array-backed seen set, yielding O(N²) time complexity. The Executor's own completion signal concedes tests were only run at N=10. Criterion #5 (performance acceptable for 100k user lists) is explicitly unmet: at N=100k, the inner loop alone executes up to 10 billion iterations. The fix is a straightforward Map-based single pass (O(N)); the current approach cannot be tuned into acceptable shape without that rewrite. No other correctness or integration defects found.

findings.description: O(N²) algorithm fails criterion #5: `seen` is a plain array, so `seen.includes()` is O(N) per outer iteration, and the inner for-loop is another O(N) per unique email — total O(N²). At N=100k this is ~10 billion string comparisons; expect multi-second stall even on fast hardware.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a single-pass Map<string, number>: iterate once to build frequency counts, then filter entries with count >= 2 — O(N) time, O(N) space.

findings.description: `other.email.toLowerCase()` is called on every inner-loop iteration, repeating normalization work proportional to N for every unique email processed.
findings.suggestion: Pre-normalize all emails into a Map during the single-pass rewrite; each email is lowercased exactly once.

findings.description: Test file listed in filesChanged but not present in the diff; test coverage cannot be verified. Executor explicitly states tests only cover N=10, and criterion #5 (100k performance) has no corresponding test.
findings.suggestion: Add a performance assertion test: generate 100k users with known duplicate distribution and assert detectDuplicates() completes within an acceptable wall-time bound (e.g. < 200ms).

fullReview.summary: The logic is correct — case normalization, empty-input handling, and the count >= 2 gate all work. However, the implementation uses a nested loop with an array-backed seen set, yielding O(N²) time complexity. The Executor's own completion signal concedes tests were only run at N=10. Criterion #5 (performance acceptable for 100k user lists) is explicitly unmet: at N=100k, the inner loop alone executes up to 10 billion iterations. The fix is a straightforward Map-based single pass (O(N)); the current approach cannot be tuned into acceptable shape without that rewrite. No other correctness or integration defects found.

criteriaAssessment.evidence: `export function detectDuplicates(...)` at line 6 of the new file.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns array of { email, count } for emails appearing 2+ times
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `Duplicate` interface defines `{ email: string; count: number }`. The `count >= 2` guard at line 19 ensures only duplicates are returned.
Outer for-loop body never executes on empty array; `result` initializes to `[]` and is returned as-is.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Handles case-insensitive email matching (foo@x.com and FOO@X.COM are duplicates)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: `user.email.toLowerCase()` used for both deduplication tracking (line 10) and inner comparison (line 17).

Nested loop is O(N²). `seen.includes()` is O(N) per outer iteration (array linear scan). Inner loop is O(N) per unique email. Executor explicitly states only N=10 was tested. At N=100k this is computationally unacceptable.
